title: Financial Examiner

rationales.taskAutomation: AI accelerates data review during bank and institution exams, but enforcement judgment, regulatory interpretation, and examiner sign-off are statutorily human responsibilities.
rationales.toolMaturity: Data-analytics tools for exam prep exist but are not yet purpose-built or mature for regulatory examination work specifically.
rationales.adoption: Limited: government and regulatory examination functions adopt new tooling slowly given statutory and procedural constraints.
rationales.agenticExposure: Compiling exam data and flagging exceptions is agent-ownable prep work; the certified finding and enforcement action are not.

tasksAtRisk: Financial data compilation and exception flagging
Standard compliance checklist review during exams
Routine call-report and filing data verification
tasksGrowing: Examining novel risks (crypto, AI-driven lending) at institutions
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2061 (Financial Examiners), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Examination data review is getting faster with AI, but the statutory finding and enforcement decision stay with a certified examiner, and new risk categories like crypto and AI-driven lending are expanding the job, not shrinking it.
